title: Silky Malaysian Freekeh Bowl
description: A savory, silky bowl that blends smoky freekeh with fragrant lemongrass, coconut cream, and a touch of fermented tamari for a quick, halal-friendly Malaysian-inspired meal.
instructions: Rinse freekeh and soak in warm water for 30 minutes; drain and set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add sliced lemongrass, ginger garlic paste, diced shallot, and red chili; sauté until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and coconut cream, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Stir in the drained freekeh and cook, uncovered, for 10–12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the freekeh has absorbed most of the liquid and the sauce is silky.
Add tamari, palm sugar, and lime juice; stir until the sugar has fully dissolved and the sauce is well combined.
Taste and adjust seasoning—more tamari for saltiness or more lime juice for brightness.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the bowl sit for 2 minutes to thicken slightly.
Transfer the freekeh mixture to a serving dish or individual bowls.
Sprinkle chopped coriander and crushed roasted peanuts over the top.
Serve immediately, optionally with thinly sliced cucumber or a splash of extra coconut milk.
